name: RBBB - Right Bundle Branch Block
identifyBy: RSR' pattern in V1 ('rabbit ears' or 'M' pattern)
Wide slurred S wave in I, aVL, V5–V6
Secondary T-wave inversion in V1–V3 (discordant changes)
Incomplete RBBB: same pattern but QRS 110–119ms
management: RBBB itself is usually benign - investigate for cause in new presentation
Causes: PE, right heart strain, anterior MI, congenital, idiopathic
New RBBB + chest pain: consider anterior STEMI or Brugada (check V1 morphology)
Bifascicular block (RBBB + LAFB or LPFB): cardiology referral
Trifascicular block (bifascicular + PR prolongation): high risk - pacemaker consideration
pearls: MaRRoW mnemonic: M in V1, W in V6 = RBBB (vs WiLLiaM for LBBB)
RBBB does NOT mask ischaemia as effectively as LBBB - ST changes in RBBB are more interpretable
New RBBB in acute PE: suggests right heart strain (associated with S1Q3T3)
Brugada pattern: coved ST in V1 + RBBB morphology - critically important to distinguish
Brugada vs RBBB: coved ST ≥2mm in V1–V2 = Brugada (not just RBBB)
differentials: RV hypertrophy (tall R in V1 but different clinical context)

qaNotes: Conduction abnormality pattern: QRS/axis morphology should be checked in diagnostic leads.
